mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate signals slack in the labor market, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC between UNRATE level and 20-day forward returns is non-negative, or t-test shows no significant difference in returns between high- and low-UNRATE periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.5%, it signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: After trigger, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average drawdown is less than 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust consumer demand and labor income, boosting corporate revenues and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When both series increase month-over-month, 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or Rank IC for the interaction term is below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts positively predict equity returns only when the federal funds rate is below 2% (accommodative policy); above this threshold, starts indicate overheating and future tightening, reversing the signal.
failure_condition: In the low-rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 2%), the Rank IC of HOUST on 20-day returns is not positive and significant (p > 0.05), or the conditional Sharpe ratio is lower than in the high-rate regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and signals economic vitality, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day returns is non-positive, or the mean return in periods of rising INDPRO is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4%, it signals a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate demand and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: After trigger, 20-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not below -1%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with stagnant or declining federal funds rate creates negative real rates, which represses borrowing costs and boosts asset prices, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL increases while FEDFUNDS does not, the 20-day forward return is not significantly positive, or the interaction Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (quarter-on-quarter above 2.5%) signals a booming economy, but when such growth occurs late in the cycle it can foreshadow overheating and policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns in the subsequent week due to mean reversion fears.
failure_condition: In the defined regime, 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or the average return is greater than 0%.
